title: Self-evolving AI, robot fights, new GPT voice, new local image model, Gemma upgrade: AI NEWS
summary: The AI Search channel reviews numerous recent AI releases and developments including image generation models, video synthesis, language model upgrades, robotic systems, and voice APIs. The episode covers 25+ AI tools and techniques released or updated in the period, highlighting breakthroughs in self-evolving systems, multi-token prediction, and embodied AI.
writeup: A weekly AI news roundup covering five or more model releases from a single week: a 3D scene reconstruction system, the new leading open-source image generator, an intrinsic video decomposition model, and Google's speculative-decoding speedup for Gemma 4. The through-line is the breadth and pace of open-source releases, with code published for nearly every result shown.
key_points: RecGen reconstructs complete 3D geometry, textures, and positions of multiple objects from one or a few RGBD images, trained on nearly 200,000 high-quality 3D assets and over 3 million synthetic depth images, outperforming SAM3D on occluded-object benchmarks where lower score means better.

HiDream 01 image by Vivago AI is a 32 GB end-to-end pixel-space model with no VAE, built-in reasoning for complex layouts, supports up to 2048x2048 resolution, and ranks as the top open-source image generator on the Artificial Analysis independent leaderboard, ahead of Wan image and SD3 turbo.

UniVid X decomposes any input video into albedo, irradiance, surface normals, and an alpha channel using two separate 800 MB models, enabling relighting, background replacement, and character compositing in post-production.

Google's multi-token prediction for Gemma 4 uses speculative decoding, pairing the main model with a smaller drafter that predicts tokens ahead so the large model can verify and accept entire sequences at once, achieving up to 3.1x speedup and nearly 80 tokens per second with no degradation in output quality.
